senses.definition: an earthquake, storm, or any other natural event that humans cannot prevent or control

examples.x: The company was not held liable for the damage to the building as it was deemed an act of God due to the severe storm.
The airline informed the passengers that the flight cancellation was due to an act of God, as the airport was closed due to heavy snowfall.
It's going to rain cats and dogs.
We were planning a picnic, but it started raining cats and dogs, so we had to postpone it.
It rained cats and dogs, and the streets were flooded.
I got completely drenched when it started raining cats and dogs on my way home.
The rain was coming down in buckets, like it was raining cats and dogs, during the outdoor concert.
After walking for hours in the snowstorm without proper protection, I was chilled to the bone and desperately sought warmth.
The icy wind cut through their clothing, leaving them frozen to the bone and longing for shelter.
She stood outside in the freezing rain, shivering and chilled to the bone within minutes.
The mountaineers reached the summit, where the biting cold air left them frozen to the bone despite their layered clothing.
The winter night was so frigid that even with a heavy coat, I felt chilled to the bone within moments of stepping outside.

senses.definition: (of temperature) falling below the freezing point

examples.x: After standing outside in the freezing wind, I was chilled to the marrow and couldn't stop shivering.
They ventured into the icy wilderness without proper gear and soon found themselves frozen to the marrow.
The blizzard was so severe that even with multiple layers of clothing, I felt chilled to the marrow.
The swimmer emerged from the frigid water, chilled to the marrow, but exhilarated by the experience.
The mountaineers traversed the treacherous peaks, their bodies becoming frozen to the marrow from the biting cold.

senses.definition: a period of hot weather between early July and early September
a period in which the weather is really warm and there is no rain

examples.x: The farmers suffered greatly during the long dry spell, as their crops wilted due to the lack of rain.
After a series of successful projects, the artist experienced a dry spell where creativity seemed elusive.
The region is currently going through a dry spell, with no rainfall for the past two months.
The team had a dry spell in scoring goals, resulting in a string of losses in their recent matches.
The author encountered a dry spell in writing, struggling to find inspiration for their next book.

senses.definition: used for saying that one does something regardless of how bad the weather is

examples.x: The mail carrier delivers the letters to our doorstep, come rain or come shine.
The dedicated gardener tends to her plants every day, come rain or come shine.
The outdoor concert will go on as scheduled, come rain or come shine, with proper arrangements for the audience's comfort.
The soccer team practices diligently, come rain or come shine, to prepare for the upcoming tournament.
We enjoy our weekly family picnic in the park, come rain or come shine, with umbrellas and raincoats as necessary.

senses.definition: a period of time in the Christmas eve when the snow has covered a huge portion of the ground

examples.x: Growing up in a tropical climate, I always dreamed of experiencing a white Christmas with snowflakes falling gently from the sky.
The children were thrilled when they woke up on Christmas morning to find a white Christmas outside their window.
Every year, we hope for a white Christmas, but it rarely snows in our region.
The postcard depicted a cozy cottage surrounded by snowy landscapes, capturing the essence of a perfect white Christmas.

senses.definition: a period marked by unusually dry and warm weather that often occurs at late autumn
used to refer to the time when the sky opens up and it starts raining heavily

examples.x: We were walking home when the heavens opened, and we got completely drenched.
The forecast didn't mention rain, but suddenly, the heavens opened, catching everyone off guard.
As soon as we set up the tent, the heavens opened, and we had to scramble to keep our belongings dry.
We were enjoying the outdoor concert when the heavens opened, forcing everyone to seek shelter.
I forgot my umbrella, and of course, the heavens opened just as I stepped outside.
